what_it_does: Records a metadata-only attachment entry that links a channel memory item to an image asset via a URI.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an attachment id att-2e2f383a.
standouts: Uses an image:// URI scheme to address a design deck slide.
Extremely minimal: only attachment metadata, no caption or context.
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References external media assets through a structured URI rather than a filesystem path.
concerns: No alt text, title, or description for the image.
The target URI cannot be resolved or verified from this file alone.
No explicit relationship semantics between the attachment and the channel memory item.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that maps a design-source channel item to deck1-2026 page 08 image.
questions: What channel memory item does this attachment belong to?
What does p-08 represent — a slide, page, or artboard?
Where is the actual image file stored and how is the image:// URI resolved?
Why was this attachment added at this specific time?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file records an image attachment reference for the design-source channel, which is exactly what its fields describe.
intention_signals: Capture a durable, timestamped reference from a channel memory artifact to a specific design deck image so it can be retrieved alongside related discussion.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme for addressing design deck slides is the most distinctive element; otherwise the structure is a conventional attachment record.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped attachment record that links a channel memory artifact to an image asset, supporting later retrieval and contextual association.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record with id att-2e2f383a in the design-source channel that points to the image asset image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-08 and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to associate a design-source channel memory item with a specific image from deck1-2026 page 08 for retrieval and traceability.
